summarization.a: Plasma-enhanced ALD reactor configuration for ZrN deposition, showing TDMAZr delivery with Ar carrier gas, forming-gas plasma generation via ICP, substrate placement, pumping, and in-situ spectroscopic ellipsometry for real-time growth monitoring, enabling low-temperature, plasma-assisted, self-limiting growth.
summarization.b: Time-resolved pulse sequence for a single ZrN PEALD cycle, detailing TDMAZr precursor pulse, Ar purge, forming-gas plasma exposure, and final purge. Temporal separation of half-reactions establishes the self-limiting ALD mechanism and underpins saturation behavior observed in subsequent figures.

| t1 | ON  | OFF | OFF | ON  |

d.question: Why is the reactor design and pulse-sequence configuration shown in this figure important for achieving high-quality ZrN films?
d.answer: The reactor design and pulse-sequence configuration ensure precise temporal separation of precursor adsorption and plasma-assisted ligand removal, which is essential for self-limiting PEALD. The inclusion of forming-gas plasma enables low-temperature nitridation, while in-situ ellipsometry provides real-time control over film growth, collectively enabling uniform, conductive ZrN films suitable for advanced electronic applications.

b.question: How are the precursor delivery and plasma activation steps arranged within a single PEALD cycle?
b.answer: The TDMAZr precursor pulse and forming-gas plasma exposure occur sequentially with Ar purge steps separating them in time.

a.question: Does the figure show temporally separated precursor and plasma steps required for self-limiting PEALD growth?

c.question: Which reactor and process components shown in the figure directly enable controlled ZrN PEALD growth?
c.answer: ICP plasma source for reactive species generation, Quartz tube for plasma confinement, TDMAZr delivery with Ar carrier gas, Ar purge lines for gas-phase isolation, In-situ spectroscopic ellipsometry for real-time thickness monitoring
